voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: Bittersweet story time. I actually always kinda hated this song, but everyone in my family seemed to love it. Then a few years ago when my uncle got sick with cancer and was bedridden, we would all sing songs with him to pass the time and lift his spirits. This one easily being his favorite, it made it into the rotation pretty much every time anyone visited him. When he eventually passed away and we were laying him to rest, someone started singing this and before long we had a group of 50 something odd people all belting out Country Roads in the middle of the cemetery. Weirdest funeral I've ever been to! And while I'm still not a huge fan of the song itself, it always makes me smile and think about him whenever I hear it.

voters.comment: Depends on the song but they do make sense on the whole. This song is about wanting someone so much you want to be part of them or one with someone. "Devour them" They use some more unique imagery but still "I want to share your mouthful | I want to do all the things your lungs do so well | I'm gonna bed into you like a cat beds into a bean bag | Turn you inside out and lick you like a crisp packet ...Devour Me | If you really think that you can really stomach me.  That Minpin line refers to the book The Minpins by Ronald Dahl suggesting a desire to be a small, hidden part of the other persons world. Also, circling back watermelon sugar high is about oral sex and the pleasure of the female orgasm using fruit to convey that metaphor (watch the music video of Harry Styles eating watermelon). Granted not the most original of a pop song content given Lollipop, sugar high, etc. Etc. but still. It's pretty common to imply sexual feelings and acts on the radio without being direct.

voters.name: Steve
voters.comment: Bittersweet story time. I actually always kinda hated this song, but everyone in my family seemed to love it. Then a few years ago when my uncle got sick with cancer and was bedridden, we would all sing songs with him to pass the time and lift his spirits. This one easily being his favorite, it made it into the rotation pretty much every time anyone visited him. When he eventually passed away and we were laying him to rest, someone started singing this and before long we had a group of 50 something odd people all belting out Country Roads in the middle of the cemetery. Weirdest funeral I've ever been to! And while I'm still not a huge fan of the song itself, it always makes me smile and think about him whenever I hear it.

That Minpin line refers to the book The Minpins by Ronald Dahl suggesting a desire to be a small, hidden part of the other persons world. Also, circling back watermelon sugar high is about oral sex and the pleasure of the female orgasm using fruit to convey that metaphor (watch the music video of Harry Styles eating watermelon). Granted not the most original of a pop song content given Lollipop, sugar high, etc. Etc. but still. It's pretty common to imply sexual feelings and acts on the radio without being direct.
